repo_description: Storybook is the industry standard workshop for building, documenting, and testing UI components in isolation
issue_title: [Bug]: @storybook/nextjs-vite next/link mock calls preventDefault before onClick
video_angle: Next.js Link mock calls preventDefault before user onClick
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

When using @storybook/nextjs-vite, the mocked next/link implementation calls event.preventDefault() before invoking the provided onClick handler.

The issue appears to be in the next/link mock [here](https://github.com/storybookjs/vite-plugin-storybook-nextjs/blob/main/src/plugins/next-mocks/alias/link/index.tsx).

The relevant code is:

```tsx
const handleClick = (e: React.MouseEvent<HTMLAnchorElement>) => {
   e.preventDefault();
   onClick?.(e);
   linkAction(hrefString, { replace, scroll, shallow, prefetch, locale });
 };
```

This can break any user-defined or composed onClick handler that checks event.defaultPrevented to decide whether to continue. One concrete example is Headless UI’s Button as={Link}. Headless UI passes a merged onClick handler to the rendered component, and that merged handler exits early if event.defaultPrevented is already true. Because the Storybook next/link mock calls preventDefault() before forwarding the event to onClick, the Headless UI merged handler receives an already-prevented event and exits before reaching the user-provided onClick.

As a result, clicking a Headless UI Button rendered as a Next.js Link does not trigger the user’s onClick handler in Storybook.

The same component works correctly in the actual Next.js app, where the real next/link implementation is used.

### Reproduction link

https://github.com/jan-dal/storybook-reproduction

### Reproduction steps

1. Clone the reproduction repository and run `pnpm i`
2. Run `pnpm storybook`
3. Open `LinkMockReproduction -> Reproduction`
4. Observe that the first two controls update their status to `Clicked`, but Headless Button as Next Link stays `Not clicked`.

### System
